name: Fufu
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 1 1/3 liters of water to a rapid boil in a large, heavy pot. Combine the two ingredients and add them to the water.
2. Stir constantly for 10-15 minutes using a strong implement such as a thick wooden spoon. The mixture will become very thick and difficult to stir; it may be easier to use one person to hold the pot while another person stirs vigorously.
3. When the fufu is ready, scoop about a cup of the mixture into a wet bowl and mix until it forms a smooth ball. Serve on a large platter alongside soup or stew.
Author's Notes:
From African News Cookbook: African Cooking for Western Kitchens, Africa News Service, Inc., edited by Tami Hultman, Penguin Books (Viking Press), ISBN 0-14 046.751 3 (pbk).
Conventional West African fufu is made by boiling starchy foods such as cassava, yam, plantain or rice, then pounding them into a glutinous mass in a giant wooden mortar and pestle. This adaptation for North Americans may be challenging if you prefer minimally processed foods, but it's worth trying at least once with West African groundnut stews.
Difficulty: moderate
Precision: measure ingredients
